factual-accuracy.requirement: States manufacturing cloud-centric baseline latency as 200-1000ms or includes 300ms or similar multi-hundred millisecond baseline
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response states that cloud-based predictive maintenance in manufacturing has a latency profile of “500 to 3,000 milliseconds of round-trip latency,” which is a multi-hundred millisecond baseline within the required 200–1000ms-or-similar range.

factual-accuracy.requirement: States manufacturing edge-distributed latency as 5-50ms or 10-50ms achieving 80-95% reduction
factual-accuracy.explanation: In the manufacturing predictive maintenance section, the response states edge anomaly-detection latency is “5–50 milliseconds” and also cites “80–95%” bandwidth reduction via deadbanding, which matches the required latency range and reduction percentage.

factual-accuracy.requirement: Demonstrates manufacturing availability improvement through edge-distributed architecture (e.g., offline operation capability, local failover, reduced dependency on cloud connectivity)
factual-accuracy.explanation: The response explicitly links edge-distributed architectures to improved availability via autonomous/offline operation during network or cloud outages, including manufacturing (“operate independently of cloud connectivity,” “during network outages”), AMRs (“no dependency on factory Wi‑Fi,” Greengrass disconnected operation), and energy distribution (“continued monitoring and control” during communication loss, reduced single point of failure).

factual-accuracy.explanation: In the AMRs section under “Quantified Impacts → Latency Improvements,” the response explicitly states “Edge computing latency | 1–10 ms,” in the context of AMR control loops and obstacle avoidance/perception tasks.

factual-accuracy.explanation: The response states bandwidth reduction figures exceeding 90% (e.g., “bandwidth savings exceeding 90%” and “70–95% reduction”), which satisfies the requirement for stating AMR bandwidth reduction as 90%+.
